title: Utah/Idaho Baptist Convention Calls UT-ID Governors, Legislature to Abolish Abortion
author: James Silberman
orientation: Reports the unanimous passage of the Resolution on Abolishing Abortion at the Utah-Idaho Southern Baptist Convention's October 2021 annual meeting, which named specific Utah, Idaho, and federal officials and demanded they pass legislation immediately abolishing abortion without exception. The article frames the vote as continued momentum from the 2021 SBC national convention and a direct rebuke to pro-life officeholders who have refused to act on Idaho's existing abolition bill.
audiences: Southern Baptist pastors and messengers weighing whether to bring abolition resolutions to their own state conventions
Idaho and Utah voters and constituents who want to know what their elected officials are being asked to do
Pro-life activists evaluating the difference between incremental regulation and immediate abolition
Abolitionist organizers tracking state-by-state ecclesial and legislative progress
Skeptics and pro-choice readers who want to understand the theological and legal arguments being made

questions.question: What did the Utah-Idaho Southern Baptist Convention actually pass, and how contested was the vote?
questions.audience: Southern Baptist pastors and messengers weighing whether to bring abolition resolutions to their own state conventions
questions.answer: The convention passed the Resolution on Abolishing Abortion (an adaptation of the 2021 national SBC resolution) with 79 cosigners, no substantive amendments from the Resolutions Committee, no opposition from any messenger on the floor, and a unanimous vote according to the resolution's drafter, Idaho pastor Paul Thompson. This stands in sharp contrast to other state conventions where similar resolutions have provoked floor fights between abolitionists and pro-lifers.
questions.quote: While abolitionist resolutions in other states have produced fierce debates on the floors of conventions between abolitionists and pro-lifers, the Utah-Idaho resolution sailed through without any substantial edits from the Resolutions Committee nor opposition from a single messenger.

questions.question: Which specific officials does the resolution name, and what exactly does it ask them to do?
questions.audience: Idaho and Utah voters and constituents who want to know what their elected officials are being asked to do
questions.answer: The resolution calls on Utah Governor Spencer Cox, Utah House Speaker Brad Wilson, Utah Senate President J. Stuart Adams, Idaho Governor Brad Little, Idaho House Speaker Scott Bedke, and Idaho Senate President Pro Tempore Chuck Winder, along with their respective legislatures, to pass, sign, and enforce legislation that would fully and immediately abolish abortion in their states without exception or compromise. It makes the same demand of President Joe Biden, Speaker Nancy Pelosi, and Senate President Pro Tempore Charles Schumer at the federal level.
questions.quote: Resolved, that as Southern Baptists we will engage, with God’s help, in establishing equal justice and protection for preborn children by calling upon Utah Governor Spencer Cox, Utah House Speaker Brad Wilson and the rest of the Utah State House, and Utah Senate President J. Stuart Adams and the rest of the Utah Senate to pass, sign, and enforce legislation that would fully, finally, and immediately abolish the heinous act of abortion in Utah immediately without exception or compromise

questions.question: What scriptural and theological foundation does the resolution lay before getting to its political demands?
questions.audience: Southern Baptist pastors and messengers weighing whether to bring abolition resolutions to their own state conventions
questions.answer: The resolution grounds its argument in the sufficiency of Scripture, the image of God in every human from fertilization, the equal worth of all humans regardless of circumstance, and God's establishment of civil authorities as His servants tasked with punishing evildoers. It explicitly cites the Baptist Faith and Message Article XV affirming life from conception and Southern Baptists' mandate to speak for the unborn.
questions.quote: Whereas, from the moment of fertilization, all humans are created in God’s image by, through, and for Jesus to the glory of God, and all souls belong to Him (Genesis 1:27; 4:1; 21;2 Isaiah 7:14; Colossians 1:16; Romans 11:36; Ezekiel 18:4)

questions.question: What makes this resolution different from a typical pro-life church statement?
questions.audience: Pro-life activists evaluating the difference between incremental regulation and immediate abolition
questions.answer: Silberman identifies the distinguishing feature as its naming of specific public officials in each state and the federal government and demanding they act immediately. Rather than deploring abortion in the abstract, it puts named governors, speakers, and senate leaders on the record as the ones being called to obedience, and rejects exceptions or compromise.
questions.quote: The distinctive feature of this resolution is its call for specific public officials in each state and the federal government to work to abolish abortion.

questions.question: Why is the article so pointed about Idaho's existing abolition bill and its leadership?
questions.audience: Idaho and Utah voters and constituents who want to know what their elected officials are being asked to do
questions.answer: Because Idaho already has a vehicle. State Representative Heather Scott has filed an abolition bill in each of the past three sessions, and Silberman charges that Governor Brad Little, House Speaker Scott Bedke, and Senate President Chuck Winder have done nothing to advance it. The Utah-Idaho SBC resolution gives those officials a religious constituency of 170 churches publicly demanding they stop stalling.
questions.quote: This is particularly applicable in Idaho where State Representative Heather Scott has filed an abolition bill each of the past three sessions. Governor Brad Little, House Speaker Scott Bedke, and Senate President Chuck Winder have not lifted a finger to support it thus far.

questions.question: Does the resolution leave any room for the standard pro-life exceptions (rape, incest, life of the mother)?
questions.audience: Pro-life activists evaluating the difference between incremental regulation and immediate abolition
questions.answer: No. It explicitly rejects any position that allows exceptions to what it calls the Holy and Righteous standard of Justice, asserting that no human institution has permission from God to allow the intentional shedding of innocent blood. This is a deliberate break from the exception-laden bills typical of mainstream pro-life legislation.
questions.quote: Resolved, that we, the messengers of the Utah/Idaho Southern Baptist Convention meeting in Meridian, Idaho October 29, 2021, agree with God that no human institution, government, rule, or authority has permission from God to allow the intentional shedding of innocent blood and we reject any position that allows for any exceptions to the Holy and Righteous standard of Justice

questions.question: How does Scott Herndon situate this resolution within the longer trajectory of Idaho abolitionism?
questions.audience: Abolitionist organizers tracking state-by-state ecclesial and legislative progress
questions.answer: Herndon, the director of Abolish Abortion Idaho and author of the state's abolition bill, traces a five-year arc: a 2016 abolitionist ballot initiative, the first abolition bill in 2019, an abolitionist Republican platform in 2020, and the 2021 SBC national resolution. The Utah-Idaho convention vote is presented as the next link in that chain, escalating from grassroots pressure to formal ecclesial demand on named officials.
questions.quote: He also tracked the progress of the movement in Idaho from an abolitionist ballot initiative in 2016, to the first abolition bill in 2019, to the making the Republican platform abolitionist in 2020, to the SBC victory in 2021.

questions.question: What indictment does Herndon level against 48 years of conventional pro-life legislative strategy in Idaho?
questions.audience: Skeptics and pro-choice readers who want to understand the theological and legal arguments being made
questions.answer: Herndon argues the traditional pro-life approach of capitulating to court precedent has produced zero abortions banned in Idaho while tens of thousands of children have died. He frames the human cost in immediate terms (29 children per week) to argue that further delay by Bedke and Winder is itself culpable inaction, not prudence.
questions.quote: 29 children a week die while we tarry, and 48 years of traditional pro-life legislative efforts that capitulate to unconstitutional court tyranny have only resulted in NO abortions banned and tens of thousands of dead Idaho babies.

questions.question: How does the resolution address the role of pastors and churches, not just civil officials?
questions.audience: Southern Baptist pastors and messengers weighing whether to bring abolition resolutions to their own state conventions
questions.answer: Beyond the demands on government, the resolution charges pastors and churches to use preaching, teaching, and community leadership to direct people toward loving God and their preborn neighbor. It then closes with a pledge that the messengers themselves stand ready to act in rescue, citing Proverbs 24:11.
questions.quote: Resolved that we encourage pastors and churches to use their God-ordained gifts of preaching, teaching, and leading our communities to love the Lord our God with all our heart, soul, mind, and strength, and to love our neighbor — yes, even our pre-born neighbor — as if he/she were our own life

questions.question: What does the resolution claim was already established by the 2021 national SBC meeting?
questions.audience: Abolitionist organizers tracking state-by-state ecclesial and legislative progress
questions.answer: The Utah-Idaho resolution treats the 2021 national SBC's Resolution on Abolishing Abortion as already settled denominational policy, asserting that the messengers there declared abortion must be immediately abolished and that preborn children must receive equal protection of the law under both Scripture and the U.S. Constitution. Utah-Idaho is positioned as enforcing and applying that earlier vote at the state level.
questions.quote: Whereas, the messengers of the 2021 Southern Baptist Convention Annual Meeting affirmed and declared, the Southern Baptist Resolution on Abolishing Abortion, that abortion must be immediately abolished and preborn children must be afforded the equal protection of the law in accordance with God’s Word and the U.S. Constitution

related.title: Abolitionists Pass Resolution*, Make Huge Strides at SBC Convention
related.reason: The Utah-Idaho resolution is explicitly modeled on the 2021 national SBC resolution covered in this article, providing the parent vote that the state convention is now applying.

related.title: Arkansas Baptists Pass Resolution on Abolishing Abortion at State Convention
related.reason: Sister state-convention vote from the same October 2021 wave, useful for comparing how different state SBC bodies adapted the national resolution.

related.title: SBC Pastors Publish "Resolution on Abolishing Abortion" for 2021 Convention Meeting
related.reason: Documents the original drafting and submission of the resolution that Paul Thompson later adapted for Utah-Idaho.

related.title: The Rise and Progress of Abortion Abolitionism in the Southern Baptist Convention
related.reason: Provides the historical arc inside the SBC that contextualizes why the Utah-Idaho vote is treated as continued momentum rather than an isolated event.

related.title: Pro-Life Idaho State Rep: "We all know nullification doesn't happen in reality."
related.reason: Background on the Idaho pro-life legislative establishment that the resolution is now publicly pressuring by name.

related.title: Pro-Life Idaho Rep. Brent Crane Insults and Intimidates Family, Promises to Keep Abortion Legal
related.reason: Concrete prior coverage of Idaho pro-life leadership obstructing abolition, illustrating the inaction Silberman and Herndon are calling out.

related.title: Oklahoma Baptists Pass Resolution Demanding an Immediate "End" to Abortion
related.reason: Earlier (2019) state-level Baptist resolution showing the multi-year ecclesial pattern that made the 2021 Utah-Idaho vote possible.
